1.1: From Paul, Silas, and Timothy. To the church of the Thessalonians—a community rooted in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Grace and peace to you.
1.2: We thank God for all of you continually, and we keep bringing you before him in our prayers.
1.3: Before our God and Father, we constantly recall the work your faith produces, the effort your love drives, and the endurance that comes from your hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
1.4: Brothers and sisters, we know that God loves you and has chosen you—
1.5: because our message came to you not just as words, but with real power—the Holy Spirit brought deep conviction with it. You saw how we lived among you, and it was all for your benefit.
1.6: You followed our example—and the Lord's—by receiving the word in the middle of great suffering, yet with the joy the Holy Spirit gives.
1.7: As a result, you became a model for all the believers throughout Macedonia and Achaia.
1.8: The Lord's message has echoed out from you—not just across Macedonia and Achaia, but everywhere. People are talking about your faith in God. We don't even need to mention it,
1.9: because people everywhere are telling the story themselves—how you welcomed us, and how you turned away from idols to serve the living and true God.
1.10: And you are waiting for his Son to come from heaven—Jesus, whom God raised from the dead, who rescues us from the wrath that is coming.

2.1: You know this yourselves, brothers and sisters—our visit to you was not a failure.
2.2: We had just been beaten and humiliated in Philippi—as you know—but our God gave us courage to tell you his good news despite fierce resistance.
2.3: The message we bring to you doesn't come from delusion, selfish ambition, or any attempt to deceive you.
2.4: We have been approved by God and entrusted with the good news—so we speak not to win human approval but to please God, who examines every heart.
2.5: You know we never used flattery—and we never hid behind smooth words to cover our greed. God is our witness.
2.6: We were not looking for praise from anyone—not from you, not from anyone else—even though as apostles of Christ we had the right to make demands of you.
2.7: Instead, we were gentle with you—like a nursing mother tenderly caring for her own babies.
2.8: We came to love you so much that we were glad to share not only God's good news with you but our very lives. You had become that precious to us.
2.9: Brothers and sisters, you remember our hard work—laboring night and day so that we would not be a financial burden to any of you while we proclaimed God's good news.
2.10: You are witnesses—and so is God—of how holy, righteous, and blameless our conduct was toward you who believed.
2.11: You know that we treated each of you the way a father treats his own children—
2.12: encouraging, comforting, and urging you to live lives that honor God, who calls you into his kingdom and glory.
2.13: We thank God constantly for this: when you heard the message from us, you didn't receive it as a merely human word—you accepted it as what it truly is, the word of God. And it is actively working in you who believe.
2.14: Brothers and sisters, you followed the example of the churches of God in Judea that are in Christ Jesus—because you suffered the same things from your own countrymen that those churches suffered from the Jewish people.
2.15: Those who killed the Lord Jesus and the prophets, who drove us out—they displease God and stand against all humanity.
2.16: In their effort to stop us from speaking to the Gentiles the good news of salvation, they keep heaping up their sins. But God's wrath has caught up with them at last.
2.17: Brothers and sisters, when we were suddenly torn away from you—in body, not in heart—we felt like orphans separated from their family. Our longing to see you again was overwhelming.
2.18: We wanted to come to you—I, Paul, tried more than once—but Satan stopped us.
2.19: After all, what is our hope, our joy, the crown we will boast about when we stand before our Lord Jesus at his coming? It's you.
2.20: You are our glory and our joy.

3.1: When we could stand the separation no longer, we decided to stay in Athens by ourselves
3.2: and send Timothy to you. He is our brother, a fellow worker in God's service for the gospel of Christ. We sent him to strengthen you and encourage your faith,
3.3: so that none of you would be shaken by these trials. You know perfectly well that this is our calling—
3.4: Even while we were still with you, we told you we would face persecution. And that is exactly what happened, as you well know.
3.5: That's why, when I couldn't stand the uncertainty any longer, I sent Timothy to find out how your faith was holding up. I was afraid the tempter might have gotten to you and made our work worthless.
3.6: But now Timothy has just returned to us from you, and he brought wonderful news about your faith and love. He tells us that you have fond memories of us and that you long to see us as much as we long to see you.
3.7: So in the middle of all our distress and hardship, brothers and sisters, your faith encouraged us.
3.8: We really feel alive now—as long as you are standing firm in the Lord.
3.9: How can we ever thank God enough for you? The joy you bring us before our God—we can barely put it into words.
3.10: Day and night we pray with all our heart to see you again and to fill in whatever is still lacking in your faith.
3.11: May our God and Father himself, and our Lord Jesus, open the way for us to come to you.
3.12: And may the Lord make your love for one another—and for all people—grow and overflow, just as our love for you does.
3.13: May he strengthen your hearts so that you will stand blameless and holy before our God and Father when our Lord Jesus comes with all his holy people.

4.1: Finally, brothers and sisters, we taught you how to live in a way that pleases God—and that is how you are living. Now we urge you in the Lord Jesus to keep doing this more and more.
4.2: You know the instructions we gave you by the authority of the Lord Jesus.
4.3: God's will for you is your sanctification—that you stay away from sexual immorality;
4.4: that each of you should learn to control your own body in a way that is holy and honoring to God,
4.5: not driven by lustful passion like people who don't know God.
4.6: No one should wrong a brother or sister or take advantage of them in this area. The Lord punishes all who do such things—we told you this before and warned you about it.
4.7: God didn't call us to an impure life—he called us to a holy one.
4.8: So anyone who disregards this instruction isn't rejecting a human rule—they are rejecting God himself, who gives you his very own Holy Spirit.
4.9: Now, about loving your fellow believers: you don't need us to write to you about this, because God himself has taught you to love one another.
4.10: And that's exactly what you are doing—loving all the believers throughout Macedonia. We urge you, brothers and sisters, to keep doing this more and more.
4.11: Make it your aim to live a quiet life, take care of your own affairs, and work with your own hands—as we directed you.
4.12: This way your life will earn the respect of those outside the faith, and you will not be dependent on anyone.
4.13: Brothers and sisters, we don't want you to be in the dark about those who have died. We don't want you to grieve the way people grieve who have no hope.
4.14: Because we believe that Jesus died and rose again, we also believe that God will bring with Jesus all who have fallen asleep in him.
4.15: Here is what we tell you by the word of the Lord: We who are still alive at the coming of the Lord will not go ahead of those who have already died.
4.16: The Lord himself will come down from heaven with a commanding shout—with the voice of the archangel and the blast of God's trumpet. And those who have died in Christ will rise first.
4.17: Then we who are still alive will be swept up together with them in the clouds to welcome the Lord in the air. And we will be with the Lord forever.
4.18: So use these words to encourage one another.

5.1: Brothers and sisters, you don't need us to write to you about the timing and circumstances—
5.2: you already know very well that the day of the Lord will arrive without warning, like a thief in the night.
5.3: While people are saying everything is safe and secure, sudden destruction will fall on them—as suddenly as labor pains on a pregnant woman. There will be no escaping it.
5.4: But you, brothers and sisters, are not living in darkness—this day will not catch you off guard like a thief.
5.5: You are all children of the light and of the day—not of the night or the darkness.
5.6: So let's not sleep like the others do—let's be wide awake and clear-headed.
5.7: Sleeping and getting drunk happen at night.
5.8: But since we belong to the day, let's be clear-headed. Let's put on faith and love as our armor—a breastplate—and make the hope of salvation our helmet.
5.9: God did not destine us for wrath—he destined us to receive salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.
5.10: He died for us so that whether we are alive or have already died, we will live together with him.
5.11: So keep encouraging one another and building each other up—as you are already doing.
5.12: Brothers and sisters, we ask you to appreciate those who work hard among you—those who lead you in the Lord and correct you when needed.
5.13: Give them deep respect and love because of their work. Live at peace with one another.
5.14: Brothers and sisters, we urge you: warn those who are lazy, encourage the timid, help those who are weak—and be patient with everyone.
5.15: Make sure no one retaliates—wrong for wrong. Always pursue what is good, both for each other and for everyone.
5.18: Give thanks in every situation—this is God's will for you in Christ Jesus.
5.20: Don't dismiss what the Spirit says through prophets.
5.21: But test everything—hold on to what is good.
5.22: Stay away from every kind of evil.
5.23: May God himself—the God of peace—make you completely holy. May your whole spirit, soul, and body be kept blameless until the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
5.24: The one who calls you is faithful—he will do what he promised.
5.25: Brothers and sisters, pray for us.
5.26: Greet all the brothers and sisters with a holy embrace.
5.27: I solemnly charge you before the Lord: make sure this letter is read to all the brothers and sisters.
5.28: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
